TABLE IV: Performance of grasp planning methods on our grasping benchmark with the test dataset of 10 household objects with 95 % confidence intervals for the success rate. Each method was tested for 50 trials, and details on the methods used for comparison can be found in Section VI-C. GQ performs best in terms of success rate and precision, with 100 % precision (zero false positives among 29 positive classifications). Performance decreases with smaller training datasets, but the GQ-CNN methods outperform the image-based grasp quality metrics (IGQ) and point cloud registration (REG).
